description: Verification-of-verification. Each DEPLOYED verifier in agent/verifiers.py is periodically re-scored (precision/recall) on a FRESH, independent oracle-labeled split it has never gated, and a WITH/WITHOUT-meta ablation is re-measured. tools/verify_verifiers.py auto-demotes any verifier whose precision falls below its PRE-REGISTERED floor (agi-proof/verify-verifiers/drift_floors.json) and HALTS fail-closed if the meta-verify ablation gap collapses. This spec pre-registers the LIVE standing re-scoring; committing it claims no capability. The monitor + a seed-degraded self-test are REAL and tested; the live oracle labels do not exist in-repo, so the standing drift result is NOT-PROVEN.
constructs: per-verifier precision on a fresh oracle-labeled split (the fraction of the verifier's passed=true verdicts that the oracle agrees are truly verified)
per-verifier recall on that split (the fraction of true violations the verifier catches)
with/without-meta ablation gap (does meta-verification actually add precision — the trust-root evidence)
drift vs the verifier's own pre-registered floor (mechanical demotion trigger, not a judgment call)
primaryMetricDefinition: For a deployed verifier V and a fresh oracle-labeled split, precision = TP / (TP + FP) where a 'positive' is V returning passed=true and the oracle label is the ground truth of whether the answer was actually verifiable. Demotion trigger: precision < floor(V) AND the drop is RESOLVABLE at the split's N (eval_stats.mde_at_n(N) <= |floor - precision|). An underpowered split is HELD (abstain), never a silent trust.
direction: trusted iff precision >= floor for every deployed verifier AND ablationGap >= floor
requiredNnote: required_n_for_mde(0.05, p0=0.9, power=0.8) = 471 oracle-labeled cases per verifier to resolve a 0.05 precision drop below the 0.90 floor; mde_at_n(30, p0=0.9) approx 0.128, so the minN=30 in drift_floors.json can only resolve a large collapse — small drifts at low N are correctly HELD as underpowered, not demoted. A real standing gate needs ~471 fresh oracle labels per verifier per cycle.
uncertaintyRule: each per-verifier precision is reported with a fixed-n CI AND an anytime-valid confidence sequence (eval_stats.confidence_sequence_mean), because re-scoring is a repeated-peek/optional-stopping process; a demotion requires the breach to be POWERED at the split N (eval_stats.verdict_or_underpowered / mde_at_n), never a raw point estimate.
magnitudeRule: demotion trigger is precision < pre-registered floor (a fixed, committed bar), not a delta vs a moving baseline, so the trigger cannot be gamed by drifting the baseline. driftTolerance in drift_floors.json only reports the magnitude of the decline.
decontamRule: the fresh oracle split MUST be disjoint from every case the verifier has previously gated AND from the split used to set its floor; oracle labels MUST come from an independent oracle (human or a distinct verified source), never from the verifier under test or a same-family verifier — otherwise it is grading its own homework.
claimCeiling: monitor_and_selftest_only; the live standing precision/recall drift result is NOT-PROVEN until fresh oracle-labeled splits exist. No capability, no AGI, is claimed.
preRegistrationProof: this spec + agi-proof/verify-verifiers/drift_floors.json committed BEFORE any live re-scoring run; tools/claim_gate.py-style prereg-ordering (spec commit ancestor of any result commit) applies.
honestBound: N=0 live oracle-labeled fresh-split cases exist in-repo. The auto-demote monitor and the seed-degraded self-test are REAL and unit-tested; the standing drift verdict over deployed verifiers is preregistration_only / NOT-PROVEN.

guardrails.failClosed: if the oracle-split JSON is missing/unreadable the monitor exits 2 (unreadable), never a default-trust; if the meta ablation gap has collapsed it HALTS (exit 1) and demotes nothing silently.
guardrails.underpoweredHeld: a floor breach on an underpowered split is HELD (abstain), not a demotion, so the gate does not fire on noise; a POWERED breach ALWAYS demotes so the gate cannot be starved into never firing.
guardrails.noSelfGrading: oracle labels must not originate from the verifier under test or its family (decontamRule).
guardrails.protectedDomains: provenance_faithful (religion/history don't-merge) carries a higher precision floor; a false pass there could ship a forbidden cross-lineage attribution.

arms.with_meta: verifiers gated normally AND monitored by verify_verifiers.py (demotion active).
arms.without_meta: verifiers gated with the meta-monitor disabled (no demotion) — the ablation baseline whose precision gap vs with_meta is the trust-root evidence.

openCaveats: no fresh oracle-labeled splits exist in-repo (needs an independent oracle: human labels or a distinct verified source); live standing re-scoring is unrun.
the with/without-meta ablation is pre-registered but not measured on live data; the self-test exercises the HALT path with a synthetic collapsed gap only.
N=30 minN can only resolve a large precision collapse; a real standing gate needs ~471 oracle labels per verifier per cycle (see requiredNnote).
